steps.title: Recognize equation types
steps.content: An equation has exactly one solution when simplification yields a unique value (conditional equation). It has no solution when it becomes a false statement (contradiction), and infinitely many when it becomes a true identity.

steps.title: Test each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — $\frac{1}{36} + x = x$: Subtract $$x$$: $$\frac{1}{36}=0$$. False - no solution. ✗
  • Choice B — $\frac{1}{36} + x = -x - \frac{1}{36}$: Add $$x$$ to both sides: $$\frac{1}{36}+2x=-\frac{1}{36}$$. Then $$2x=-\frac{2}{36}$$, so $$x=-\frac{1}{36}$$. Exactly one solution. ✓
  • Choice C — $\frac{1}{36} - x = \frac{1}{36} - x$: Both sides identical — true for all $$x$$. Infinitely many solutions. ✗
  • Choice D — $\frac{1}{36} - x = -x$: Add $$x$$: $$\frac{1}{36}=0$$. False - no solution. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
